note: Deterministic mutation operators. The three v1 operators are TOP-LEVEL only and are still applied by validate_negative_fixtures.py; R4 finding B7 was that top-level-only mutation made '208 mutants, 0 survivors' sound far stronger than it was. The eighteen v2 families are applied by scripts/validate_recursive_mutations.py, which walks every positive example part RECURSIVELY, resolving the effective schema at each node (through $ref, allOf and the applicable if/then and anyOf/oneOf branch) so it knows which fields are required, which arrays are sets, and which values are references. No randomness anywhere: mutants are enumerated in sorted path order, so the corpus and the report are byte-stable.
kill_criterion: A mutant is KILLED if the hardened JSON Schema rejects the mutated part, or if validate_cross_record_semantics.collect_issues flags the mutated bundle. Every unmutated example bundle is issue-free, so any issue a mutant raises is attributable to the mutation.

operators.description: remove each top-level required field, one at a time
set each top-level enum-constrained field to the value 'NOT-A-LEGAL-VALUE'
add one undeclared top-level field '__mutant__': true
delete each required field at EVERY depth, not only the top level
blank each required string; '' is not a declaration
empty each required array or map the contract requires to be nonempty
duplicate a member of an array-as-set, and collide two ids that must differ
repoint each currently-resolvable reference (claim, token, evidence, episode) at nothing
desynchronise an analysis version between records that must share it
desynchronise an occurrence version between records that must share it
delete the field a residual's own disposition makes mandatory
make a claim depend on itself, directly or through another claim
delete the status of a verdict the required path demands
state an episode- or claim-level pathway summary the statuses do not support
strip the reason from a not-applicable status, anywhere in the record
give one token several of_type entries (the unimplemented many-to-many MetaInst)
empty the binding map: a token with no material binding
declare a RelSpec at or after the result it is used to assess
vary a field the PerturbSpec declares invariant
collapse a candidate SET of complete profiles into one partial profile (finding B1)
overwrite a claim's reasoning adequacy with the episode-level pathway state (finding B2)
make an analysis inherit from itself, or from a parent that resolves nowhere (D1)
rename an embedded token to another episode's token id (global identity, D3)
point a token's claim scope at a claim owned by ANOTHER episode's ledger (D4)
repoint a governing-configuration mu_ref or a token of_type at an undeclared edition (D5)
add a precedence self-edge, or close a 2-cycle over declared governing types (D5)
postdate a RelSpec past the outcome using a UTC offset that string-compares earlier, or make it timezone-naive (D7)
set a token's expiry before its effective_from (D7)
strip or unresolve an audit-ready record's external_refs, or desynchronise its analysis from the bundle (D6)
drop one entry from a claim's recorded req_reason projection (omission attack, D8)

expectation: Every generated mutant is killed at a declared layer, OR is listed below as a justified equivalent with a reason why the mutation does not change what the record claims. An undeclared survivor fails the run.

justified_equivalents.reason: The residual is disposed 'validated-resolved' and carries BOTH evidence_refs and verdict_refs. The contract requires one of the two, so deleting evidence_refs leaves a record whose disposition is still fully supported by verdict_refs. This is a redundancy in the example, not a gap in the contract: deleting both IS killed (see the 'all-conditionals' mutant of family 8). (Part index re-signed #4->#6 after the R4 independent review added the two declared metaortheme records to this bundle.)

Same redundancy as above, reached through the disposition-conditional family. verdict_refs alone still discharges the 'validated-resolved' contract. (Part index re-signed #4->#6 after the R4 independent review added the two declared metaortheme records to this bundle.)

Same redundancy, other direction. evidence_refs alone still discharges the 'validated-resolved' contract. (Part index re-signed #4->#6 after the R4 independent review added the two declared metaortheme records to this bundle.)

A ledger that opens NO burdens is well-formed: not every episode leaves a residual. Emptying the residual list yields a record that makes a different but not malformed claim, and catching it would require an external source of truth about which burdens actually existed — which is outside the reference model by construction. (Part index re-signed #4->#6 after the R4 independent review added the two declared metaortheme records to this bundle.)

Emptying evidence_refs on a residual that also carries verdict_refs leaves the 'validated-resolved' contract discharged by verdict_refs. Same redundancy as the deletion mutants above. (Part index re-signed #4->#6 after the R4 independent review added the two declared metaortheme records to this bundle.)
As above: an empty residual list is a well-formed ledger. The record then claims no burdens rather than an unresolved one; nothing internal to the bundle contradicts it.

As above. This is precisely the limit the whole-state-reread example illustrates in prose: the machine floor can convict a closure claim asserted OVER a recorded unresolved burden, but it cannot know about a burden nobody recorded.

This episode declares record_mode 'minimal'. The contract lets a minimal record carry an empty evidence list precisely because it makes no audit claim; only an 'audit-ready' record must carry evidence or an explicit evidence_absent_reason. Emptying evidence here produces an honest minimal record, so there is nothing to reject.
As above: a 'minimal' record may legitimately carry no evidence. The corresponding mutation on the audit-ready record (pillar-episode) IS killed at the schema layer.

The residual is disposed 'validated-resolved' and carries BOTH evidence_refs and verdict_refs; deleting one leaves the other, which alone still discharges the disposition's conditional-field requirement. The record's claim is unchanged. Same adjudicated class as the pillar-episode residual redundancy.
A ledger that opens NO burdens is well-formed: not every episode leaves a residual, and no claim of the record changes. Same adjudicated class as the pillar-episode empty-residuals equivalent.
Emptying evidence_refs on a residual that also carries verdict_refs leaves the 'validated-resolved' disposition discharged by the verdict reference; the record's claim is unchanged.

The residual is disposed 'validated-resolved' and carries BOTH evidence_refs and verdict_refs; deleting one leaves the other, which alone still discharges the disposition's conditional-field requirement. The record's claim is unchanged. Same adjudicated class as the pillar-episode residual redundancy.

The record's claim is unchanged. Same adjudicated class as the pillar-episode residual redundancy.
